headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there is one underlined word and one gap. From the list of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word and that will, at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence.

S1.qs: 1.	Good teaching **stimulates** the interest of students, whereas bad teaching ....................... it
2.	Some youths of this generation are often **hard-hearted** compared to those of past generations who were ...............................
3.	The doctor does not think the patient will recover; the signs are **ominous** rather than ............................
4.	The purpose of education is to **enhance** progress and not to ...................... it.
5.	The teacher gave him high marks and wrote **complimentary** remarks in the margin, which surprisingly gave him .................... feelings.
6.	Many people find cod liver oil **disgusting** but soldiers find it ......................
7.	The investigators relied solely on **conjectures**, which were not as reliable as .....................
8.	It was **obvious** that Modou committed the crime, but his friends were still ......................
9.	The government was blamed for implementing policies that favoured the **elite** at the expense of the ......................
10.	His suggestion on how to deal with the thief was **invaluable**, but the police considered it .....................

S2.headd: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences.

S2.qs: 11.	The woman’s behaviour clearly shows that her love for her husband was merely ...........................
12.	The man was told in his own interest to	the ............... statement he had made.
13.	Mum made soothing remarks in order to .................dad
14.	Ebou maintained his stand in ..................... of the principal's orders
15.	The outgoing president warned his successor to beware of ........................ who praise every action of government, good or bad
16.	Salimata came into ................. when she won the beauty contest.
17.	The current economic crisis has led to the ...................
18.	We all agree that the television is a more powerful ..................... of communication than the radio
19.	He won the election by a .................. of 500 votes
20.	Daylight armed-robbery is a common ................. in big cities.

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.

S3.qs: 21.	When Joe was threatened with suspension he made a clean breast of his offence. This means that Joe

opts.q1: B. made friends with the other offenders

S3.qs: 22.	When Eunice was discharged from hospital she was a shadow of her former self. This means that she
23.	Chinwe can be stubborn yet I have a soft spot for her. This means that I

opts.q0: A. am fond of Chinwe even though she is stubborn
opts.q1: B. alone know how to control Chinwe
opts.q3: D. have a cure for her stubbornness

S3.qs: 24.	The principal is high-handed with his staff. This means that the principal

opts.q1: B. is very friendly and easy-going with his staff
opts.q2: C. shakes hands with his staff frequently
opts.q3: D. uses his authority over his staff sternly

S3.qs: 25.	The loan was taken on a gentleman's agreement. This means that

opts.q0: A. there was no documentation of the loan
opts.q1: B. two gentlemen witnessed the loan
opts.q3: D. the loan was not to be repaid

S3.qs: 26.	Constructing the road along the coast was difficult but the men had already broken the back of the work before the rains. This means that they had

opts.q1: B. completed the most difficult part of it

S3.qs: 27.	Whenever Nkoyo finds herself at a loose end, she goes visiting people. This means that Nkoyo goes visiting people whenever she

opts.q0: A. has nothing to occupy her

S3.qs: 28.	Kola will get the job because his father will surely pull strings for him. This means that his father will

opts.q1: B. remove some of his difficulties

S3.qs: 29.	To give a bronze medal to the athlete who was deliberately intercepted on the track was cold comfort. This means that the bronze medal was
30.	The students planned to confront the principal with their grievances but as they got closer to his office, they developed cold feet. This means that

opts.q0: A. they could no longer walk

S4.headd: From the words lettered A to D below each of the following sentences, choose the word or group of words that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word as it is used in the sentence.

S4.qs: 31.	The woman **stifled** a cry of astonishment as she discovered the identity of the robber.
32.	That rock is **a potential** danger to fishing boats
33.	His contribution to the development programme in the village remains **unrivalled**
34.	Members of the party agreed that some serious **thinking** is required before a final decision can be taken
35.	Our chief is very intelligent but he could be **parochial** in his judgements.
36.	All that was left after the riot were the **charred** remains of the victims.
37.	The **incessant** chatter of the pupils irritated the visitor.
38.	Musa won the debate because he **articulated** his arguments convincingly
39.	I was surprised at the **gallantry** of the Senegalese peace-keepers
40.	The clerk was dismissed for **dereliction** of duty.

S5.headd: From the word or group of words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

S5.qs: 41.	This is a bad case, .................. hard you try to defend the accused.
42.	.................. pain has been taken to make the party a success
43.	School children are advised to refrain ................ smoking
44.	The principal, as well as the teachers ............. in school
45.	Fatima ............... come to work today since she is still sick
46.	Aminata has .............. her engagement to Sule
47.	................... who knows the times will not open his door to strangers at night.
48.	All the final year students will attend ............... party
49.	My friend’s son stole the ................... briefcase.
50.	This isn’t your ring; it is your ........................
52.	I was asked whether I ................. to go on the outing
53.	Our teacher is leaving our school in July to ................ a new job
54.	The old woman had no children and no real friends ....................
55.	Certain tasks may be unpleasant at first, but gradually one may get .................... them
56.	.................... in their youth is a source of pleasurable reminiscence for elderly persons
57.	The minister told his congregation that one day ...................... would be rewarded
58.	Maram liked the exhibits at the trade fair but she really enjoyed ..................... the most
59.	The jury gave .................... verdict of guilty in the murder case
60.	The room in ............... they lived was quite large and well-lit
61.	The great gates of the ancient palace were .......................

opts.q0: A. beautiful pieces of iron works
opts.q1: B. the beautiful pieces of iron works
opts.q2: C. beautiful pieces of iron work
opts.q3: D. a beautiful piece of iron works

S5.qs: 62.	The report said that although the country was rich ..................... formed a substantial part of the population

opts.q2: C. some old and some neglected

S5.qs: 63.	I had a phone call from her yesterday, so she .............. have found out my telephone number
64.	I cannot imagine that a man ................ his standing could stoop so low as to accept bribes
65.	Lamin and Michael washed .................. but mummy washed Yeanie
66.	The principal has gone to Freetown and will come back in ....................... time.
67.	The students have not come back from their school farms ..................... ?
68.	We haven’t confirmed .................. signature this is
70.	The moon seemed to be sailing through the clouds ........................ a ship thrusting its way through a stormy ocean.
71.	The school's high jump champion has been ..................

opts.q0: A. off very much form most of the time
opts.q1: B. very much off-form most of the time
opts.q2: C. very most off-form most of the time
opts.q3: D. off-form most of the time very much

S5.qs: 72.	The new bridge was a complete waste of money and .................. built in the first place

opts.q2: C. needed not to have been
opts.q3: D. need not to have been

S5.qs: 73.	Araba took up an appointment as a part-time instructor as a means ......................... his meager income.
74.	........................ opportunity we now have to rid the nation of corruption!

S6.headd: In the following passages the numbered gaps indicate missing words. Against each number in the list below each passage four choices are offered in columns lettered A to D. Choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gap in the passages.
PA: One of the indicators of --76-- underdevelopment in this country is the backward state of the transport sector. This is typified by the poor state of the railway --77--.
In advanced countries, commuter trains carry passengers from city to city and within suburbs. The --78-- train goes a lot faster connecting major cities and even states; such trains have --79-- only.

In the intra-city trains, it is only shelter and seats that are provided. There are no --80--. In some cases, and because of the long hours of service of inter-city or inter-state trains, --81-- and --82-- are available in addition to coaches.

Some trains offer --85-- services, transporting goods. Such trains stop at specific --86-- to deliver goods and to refuel.
Most trains have --87-- to push or pull them to prevent them from turning around.
